title: dorycnium pentaphyllum var. frutescens
intro: Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. frutescens, also known as the small creeping knapweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the pea family native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's health benefits include its ability to treat various ailments such as digestive issues, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The herbal preparations of Dorycnium pentaphyllum var. frutescens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used internally or externally to promote overall well-being.
